ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a complete teriyaki salmon set meal. FOODON_00002809 (salmon food product) used as the closest parent anchor for the primary protein component.
ontology.foodex2_description: Salmon, cooked — used as the primary component anchor for this composite set meal including grilled teriyaki salmon, steamed rice, miso soup, and side salad.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a composite code for a full Japanese set meal (teishoku). A00GJ (cooked salmon) is used as the primary protein anchor. The composite nature of the dish (rice, miso soup, salad) is documented here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for teriyaki grilled salmon set meal as a composite dish.

main_ingredients: teriyaki sauce (soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar)
miso soup (miso paste, dashi, tofu, wakame seaweed)
allergens: gluten (wheat in soy sauce and miso)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal portion of approximately 550 g, comprising a grilled teriyaki salmon fillet (~150 g), a bowl of steamed white rice (~200 g), a bowl of miso soup (~150 ml), and a small side salad or pickled vegetables (~50 g).

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy: Salmon, Atlantic, farmed, cooked, dry heat (FDC ID 175168); Rice, white, short-grain, cooked (FDC ID 169756); Soup, miso (FDC ID 172451); composite estimation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for a complete teriyaki grilled salmon set meal. Per-100g values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: grilled Atlantic salmon (FDC ID 175168, ~27% of set weight), cooked white rice (FDC ID 169756, ~36% of set weight), miso soup (FDC ID 172451, ~27% of set weight), and mixed salad/pickles (~10% of set weight). Teriyaki glaze contribution to sodium and sugar is incorporated. The input source listing 'USDA FoodData Central — International Cuisine Nutritional Reference' is not a recognised USDA publication title; values are re-anchored to named SR Legacy entries as documented here.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — white rice (boiled, short-grain) analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists for a composite teriyaki salmon set meal. GI estimated at 64 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate component: cooked short-grain white rice (GI ~64–72, University of Sydney GI Database). The salmon, miso soup, and salad components contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (64 × 59.95) / 100 ≈ 38.4 g (carbohydrate per 550 g serving = 10.9 × 5.5 = 59.95 g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium from miso paste, soy-based teriyaki sauce, and dashi stock
hypertension.guidance: At 327 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1,799 mg per full 550 g set), this meal approaches or exceeds the WHO daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg in a single sitting; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium soy sauce and miso, ask for teriyaki sauce on the side, and limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic load from white rice (GI ~64, GL ~38 per set) combined with added sugars in teriyaki glaze
type2_diabetes.guidance: The white rice base delivers a glycaemic load of approximately 38 per serving, which is clinically high for type 2 diabetes management; individuals should request a brown rice or cauliflower rice substitution, ask for teriyaki sauce applied lightly, and pair the meal with the salad component first to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from salmon offset partially by high sodium and moderate saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salmon provides cardioprotective EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids (estimated 0.8–1.2 g per 100 g of the salmon component), which support cardiovascular health; however, the high sodium content (327 mg/100 g) and moderate saturated fat (1.5 g/100 g) from the composite set meal require individuals with cardiovascular disease to request reduced-sodium condiments and limit the teriyaki glaze to minimise sodium and added-sugar intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High omega-3 EPA/DHA content from salmon with anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Salmon is rich in EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids that directly reduce joint inflammation, making this meal broadly suitable for rheumatoid arthritis; however, individuals sensitive to nightshades should monitor for joint flares if the side salad contains tomato, and reduced-sodium condiments should be requested to minimise RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teriyaki Grilled Salmon Set Meal (照り焼きサーモン定食, Teriyaki Saamon Teishoku) is a Japanese-style set meal (teishoku) served at Japanese restaurants in Singapore, including the 151D restaurant chain. The meal centres on a grilled Atlantic salmon fillet coated in a sweet and savoury teriyaki glaze made from soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar, which caramelises on the fish surface during grilling to produce a glossy, umami-rich crust. The salmon is tender and flaky inside with a lightly charred exterior. The set is accompanied by a bowl of steamed short-grain white rice, a bowl of miso soup (typically containing silken tofu, wakame seaweed, and dashi stock), and a small side salad or Japanese pickles (tsukemono). Per 100 g of the composite set, the meal provides approximately 136 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 6.4 g fat, and 10.9 g carbohydrate, with sodium at 327 mg. The salmon component contributes significant omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), which are cardioprotective and anti-inflammatory. The white rice base gives the meal a medium glycaemic index of approximately 64 and a high glycaemic load of ~38 per full serving, making it a concern for type 2 diabetes management. Sodium is elevated due to miso paste, soy sauce in the teriyaki glaze, and dashi, making it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension unless reduced-sodium substitutions are requested. The meal is culturally associated with Japanese washoku dining traditions and is widely available at Japanese casual dining restaurants across Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for a complete teriyaki grilled salmon set meal. Per-100g values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for grilled Atlantic salmon, cooked white rice, and miso soup, which are individually well-supported. Confidence is medium because the exact teriyaki glaze formulation, rice-to-fish ratio, and miso soup volume vary by restaurant preparation.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: grilled Atlantic salmon (~27% of 550 g set), cooked short-grain white rice (~36%), miso soup (~27%), and mixed salad/pickles (~10%). The input source title 'USDA FoodData Central — International Cuisine Nutritional Reference' is not a recognised USDA publication; values have been re-anchored to named USDA SR Legacy entries as documented in nutrient_source. 2. Fields set to -1 (data not available from composite estimation): vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite restaurant set meal is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as a single composite entry as of 2024; individual component values exist but cannot be reliably weighted without confirmed restaurant-specific portion data. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.1 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.8 g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy grilled Atlantic salmon fatty acid profile weighted by salmon proportion in the set. 4. GI of 64 is an analogous estimate based on cooked short-grain white rice (University of Sydney GI Database); the salmon and miso components contribute negligible carbohydrate. 5. The input record listed two identical portion entries (both 550 g); a single 550 g default serving is used. 6. Halal status is not confirmed — mirin and sake (rice wine) are used in teriyaki sauce and miso preparation; the meal is not certified halal.
